frameworks.name: circular inference
frameworks.one_line: prior beliefs reverberate through feedback and corrupt sensory evidence

frameworks.name: hierarchical Bayesian inference
frameworks.one_line: hierarchical cortical inference extended with reverberating feedback loops

frameworks.name: attractor dynamics
frameworks.one_line: integrator becomes bistable attractor under corrupted evidence

frameworks.name: adapting populations with lateral inhibition
frameworks.one_line: standard neuromechanistic account of bistability dynamics

core_question: Can circular inference in a hierarchical brain explain bistable perception of ambiguous stimuli?
key_assumptions: sensory areas are modulated by feedback from higher-level areas
ambiguous input has two near-equally supported interpretations
manipulations: presence vs absence of feedback reverberation
key_findings: circular inference turns an input integrator into a bistable attractor
model switches between two highly trusted interpretations
model reproduces Levelt's laws of bistability
intermittent stimulus presentation stabilizes the percept
interpretations: bistability reflects prior beliefs corrupting evidence via feedback loops
bistable perception is a window onto generic hierarchical perceptual inference
